subtitle: Deinosuchus hatcheri (plus in-game subspecies variants)

wikiLinks.label: Path of Titans modding wiki — Deinosuchus (KTO)

seededFrom: Path of Titans community wiki (modding portal)
dataProvenance.curveNote: NexLink lists default server CurveOverrides samples for `KTODeino.*`. Prefix every line with `CurveOverrides=(CurveName="KTODeino.` under `[/Script/PathOfTitans.IGameSession]` per host documentation. Stop the server before editing.
dataProvenance.disclaimer: Game balance patches and mod updates can drift. Verify values in-game or from your server files when precision matters.

heroImageCaption: Life reconstruction (artistic; not a PoT skin). Wikimedia / Nobu Tamura.

taxon.note: Several epithets on the modding wiki are fictional ‘subspecies’ flavors for gameplay variety; D. irwini honors Steve Irwin per community trivia.

description.gameplay: A gigantic semi-aquatic playable crocodilian with crushing jaws and heavy armor. In Path of Titans the KTO Deinosuchus is roughly on the scale of the largest base-game carnivores (~14.5 m cited on community pages). Land mobility is limited like other semi-aquatics; water power, grabs, lunges, and bone-breaking charge attacks define its ambush identity.

trivia: First modded playable release from the KTO Mods team; community pages also note a separate DB Deinosuchus implementation.
The mesh is referenced against an Fadeno skeletal reconstruction of D. hatcheri on community documentation.
Community economy notes: unlocking every listed skin costs 86,300 Marks; every listed ability unlock totals 21,700 Marks (108,000 combined) — re-verify if patches change pricing.
KTO Deinosuchus is sometimes noted as omitting a D. schwimmeri-style variant that appears on another mod implementation.

marksEconomy.source: Community modding wiki trivia (confirm in live client).

abilities.name: Bone Snap
abilities.description: Charged attack; bone-break scales with how long the ability is held (community description).

abilities.name: Fisher
abilities.description: Eats meat and fish; moderate food drain.

abilities.name: Generalist
abilities.description: Fish, meat, shellfish, and bones; faster food and water drain.

abilities.name: Thick Scales
abilities.description: More armor and tighter turning at the cost of speed.

abilities.name: Basking
abilities.description: Land speed and stamina regen up; trades health.

abilities.name: Death Roll
abilities.description: Heavy roll attack usable in water.

abilities.name: Fracturing Lunge
abilities.description: Short lunge ending in a crushing bite; self-slow after use.

growthStageCaption: Five samples per curve — typical PoT growth indexing (juv → elder ordering is common; confirm on your server build).

paleontology.summary: Deinosuchus is a giant alligatoroid crocodylian from the Late Cretaceous of North America (and related forms are debated globally). It is famous for unusually deep, enlarged teeth and a broad rostrum suited to overpowering large prey in coastal rivers and estuaries.
paleontology.size: Adult length estimates in scientific literature have ranged widely depending on method; modern discussions often place large individuals in the ~8–10 m class with controversial larger estimates from fragmentary material. Treat headline ‘15 m’ figures from pop sources skeptically — they depend on extrapolation.
paleontology.fossilRecord: Remains come chiefly from Campanian marginals of the Western Interior Seaway (e.g., material historically tied to ‘Deinosuchus riograndensis’ complexes) with iconic occurrences across southern U.S. coastal-plain formations. Isolated teeth and skull fragments drive size extrapolations.

papers.cite: Schwimmer, D. R. (2002). King of the Crocodilians: The Paleobiology of Deinosuchus. Indiana University Press.
papers.note: Classic synthesis; still a starting point for paleo-histories (some taxonomy updated since).

papers.cite: Brochu, C. A. (1997). Morphology, fossils, and the phylogenetic relationships of Alligatoroidea. PhD dissertation / related papers on alligatoroid systematics framing Deinosuchus.

Cossette, A. P., & Brochu, C. A. (2020). Massive taxon-wide skull analysis reveals nuances of crocodilian evolutionary history. *Royal Society Open Science* (and related 2020 Eastgate work revising Deinosuchus species concepts).
papers.note: Illustrates how ongoing work reframes species-level taxonomy — good example of why in-game ‘subspecies’ are flavor, not peer-reviewed taxa.

collections: Look for casts and originals in major U.S. natural history museums with Western Interior Seaway halls (NMNH, AMNH outreach exhibits rotate).
